free_text: Study of alpha-gamma correlation in the reaction plane
and the mechanism of the 28Si(alpha,alpha'gamma)28Si*
reaction
U-120 cyclotron.
The beam with intensity 60 nA.
Self-supporting silicon target of the thickness
150 ug/cm2.
A silicon surface barrier detector located in
the scattering chamber was used to detect alpha
particles.
Gamma rays were detected with 3 x 3 inches
12S8/E-O Harshaw NaI(Ti) scintillation detector. Its
angular resolution was +-12 deg. The range of angles
accessible to the gamma detector was from 40 to 140 deg
SD: Corrections in all Subents.
No explanation of relative units is given.
The normalized correlation functions were calculated
from the measured number of coincidences Nc for the
random coincidences and the number of counts in the
corresponding peaks in single spectra N.
Digitizing error for ANG2
No information about source of uncertainty
Figs 6,7,8,9 from Acta Phys.Pol.,B13(1982)767
SD: SF3=A/G -> G/A in REACTION code.
STATUS updated. Accuracy of digitized data presentation
revised. ANG-SEC added.

SD: STATUS updated. Accuracy of digitized
